name: Turn-of-The-Century Molasses Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted butter or margarine (room temperature)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F with rack in center.
2. Generously grease a 12-cup-capacity bundt pan. Lightly dust with flour, tapping the pan over sink to remove excess flour.
3. Combine molasses and baking soda; set aside.
4. Use mixer to cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Add reserved molasses mixture. Mix until combined.
6. Combine flour and salt. Add alternately with milk until combined and smooth.
7. Transfer batter to prepared pan.
8. Bake until toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, about 50 minutes.
9. Set pan on cooling rack; let cool 15 minutes.
10. Gently invert pan and remove cake. Let rest at least 45 minutes before serving.
11. Serve warm. Can be made a day ahead and kept at room temperature, well-covered, or frozen up to 3 months, wrapped airtight.
12. To serve, reheat cake (thaw in wrapping first, if frozen), wrapped in foil in 350°F oven until warmed through, about 15 minutes (or alternately, in microwave oven on medium power until just warmed through, not hot).
13. Sift confectioners' sugar over cake.
